performance_overview: Through 46 sessions (inception 2026-06-01) Bull holds the same 7 open positions (SCHW, PCG, XLU, CMG, GIS, META, BMY). Account equity ~$100,370 (+0.37% since $100K inception) vs SPY -1.94% cumulative — Bull leads the benchmark by ~2.31pts, its widest gap yet. Today (Jul 17 EOD) was a down day handled well: net -$536.04 (-0.53%) while SPY fell -0.95%, a +0.42% single-day alpha with zero exits and no death crosses. Two evening no-op scans (Jul 16) plus a no-op open (Jul 17) kept the book static. Unrealized book stays net-positive but eased across the board: GIS +5.62% (best, down from +7.46%), SCHW +4.05% (anchor, down from +4.73%), BMY +3.59% (Day 1, improving), PCG +1.21% (eased); reds are XLU -1.06% (weakening), CMG -1.55% (recovered from -2.78%), and META -2.71% — META has now rolled over from flat into red, the feared extended-entry reversal materializing.
best_signals: RSI 55-60 at entry: 2/2 wins (100%), avg +2.15% — the ONLY consistently profitable bucket for the LEGACY intraday RSI strategy; n=2, thin sample, informational only now that the strategy is MA-20/60 trend-following. Do not act on it without n>=10.

Shallow-cross probation entries continue to hold up under trend-following: GIS (0.29% cross, 75% unit) still +5.62% after easing from +7.46%, and BMY (0.13% cross, 50% unit) +3.59% on Day 1. Early positive read that a heavy size-down — NOT a skip — is the right call for shallow-but-non-earnings-adjacent golden crosses. Still only 2-3 samples; keep sizing down, do not upsize yet.
avoid: Shallow EMA cross entries on earnings-adjacent names: TSLA (Jul 7 entry, 0.06% cross spread, earnings Jul 22) lost -4.78% on exit. Rule: SKIP (do not size down) when BOTH shallow cross spread (<0.5%) AND earnings within 14 days apply simultaneously. This is the live rule under the current trend strategy.

Extended entries (price well above EMA-20 at entry): META entered Jul 15 at ~8.5% above EMA-20, peaked +2.83%, and has now rolled over to -2.71%. Early evidence that chasing an extended golden cross buys the digestion/pullback — watch whether an EMA-20 retest holds or a death cross confirms. Treat large above-EMA extension as a size-down (or wait-for-pullback) caution, distinct from the shallow-cross rule. n=1, informational.

Very shallow golden crosses (<0.1% EMA-20/60 spread): NVDA (0.07%) skipped Jul 15 as a near-clone of the failed TSLA pattern (0.06%). Treat sub-0.1% crosses as noise-level, high reversal risk — skip on sight. BMY at 0.13% cleared this line by a hair, taken at half unit, and is working (+3.59%), validating 0.1% as the skip floor.

LEGACY intraday RSI buckets (informational, strategy retired): RSI 60-65 0/3, RSI 65-75 0/1, gap-up opens with RSI>60 — all losers historically. Retained only as a record of why the intraday RSI approach was abandoned in favor of trend-following.
recent_market_context: Jul 13-17: MA-20/60 trend strategy running steadily; 7 positions held, book net positive on unrealized but easing. Broad market softened two sessions running — SPY -0.82% (Jul 16) then -0.95% (Jul 17) — and Bull's low-beta/defensive-leaning book (utilities, staples, healthcare, financials) beat SPY on both down days for back-to-back positive alpha. No death crosses triggered; the Jul 16 evening scan found 0 golden crosses across 553 symbols, so no new entries queued. VIX last read 15.67 (calm) on Jul 16. check_earnings.py confirmed working (no yfinance-TLS block).

Day 5; stop $34.41. Best position in the book — eased from +7.46% to +5.62% but still the standout. 75% unit (207 of 276). Probation graduating well; keep holding, do not upsize.
